card.intuition: The set of policies and tools to enable the recovery or continuation of vital infrastructure and systems following a natural or human-induced disaster.
card.analogy: An airplane's redundant engines and emergency parachute: usually, the engines work fine. But if a bird flies into one engine (A Data Center fire), the pilot must have a plan to land safely or switch to the other engine immediately.
when_to_use: Protecting against cloud regional outages (e.g., AWS US-East-1 goes down)
Recovering from a catastrophic database deletion or ransomware attack
Meeting legal requirements for data safety in highly regulated industries
gotchas: DR is NOT Backup: a 'Backup' stores the data. 'DR' is the plan for how you get the web servers running again using that data.
Untested plans are useless: if you haven't tried a full 'Recovery' in a year, it probably won't work when the real fire starts.

exercise.prompt: Identify the acronym for the maximum amount of TIME it should take to bring a system back online after a disaster.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.upper() == "RTO"
assert check("RTO")
print("PASS")
